profile.title: Uvhash
profile.introduction: A berserker who fights relentlessly and grows stronger, becoming even more powerful as their HP drops.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Exalt deals high AoE DMG and enjoys high {STR} bonus.
When Exalt triggers {Devour}, the subsequent Exalt DMG and {STR} Buff in this battle are increased.
The lower the HP, the higher the Damage.
In this level, Uvhash will join the battle at Level 50 with Skill Level 5 and 3 Enlightenments, gaining 30% {Embryo Fusion} each turn, and enemies will be Enhanced for the first two turns.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
How could we forget the hero Uvhash? He was the finest of men. The warrior knelt in the sunset sands, his warm blood soaking him. Vultures circled above, singing his dirge: 
No one will honor you with tears, no one will bury you with cries, for you have already made your name in Rome.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Silver Core Number: B-005
Excavation Time: A.F.1@1 Year@3 Month
Excavation Location: In the countryside of Flandre, in some nameless Roman tomb ruins
Details Recorded: The Silver Core holder was named Ludwig Plin, sentenced to burning by local religious judgment due to a summoning incident. Plin claimed to have obtained this Silver Core on the battlefield of the ninth holy crusade, and then went near Brabant to hold a bloody sacrifice for summoning "The Great Unspeakable One" with "The Extraterrestrial Servants" and "The Invisible Companions".
Recorder: Ward Phillips
— "Awakener Silver Core Acquisition Report: B-005"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
"4:30 Ran 6 miles along the Isis River. Argued with a passerby elderly woman and kicked a poodle.
......
12:00 Lunch: Honey pancakes, olive appetizers, and... five servings of roast lamb? Unbelievable.
13:00 Went to the Trout Fighting Club. Record: 10 wins, 0 losses, fastest time 3 seconds—opponent forfeited out of fear.
19:00 Watched "The Shepherd King" at Covent Theatre with the Keepers, got kicked out for loud political remarks.
22:00 Found loitering near St. Marian Hospital, likely planning to steal blood packs.
Observation ends, danger level: SSS."
—Excerpt from "Hannah's Awakener Observation Notes"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Caligula's court alchemist, Marcus Antis, created the "golem" at the cost of many living people.
Due to the imperfection of the alchemical ritual, he had to drink a large amount of blood daily. He killed political enemies for Caligula and helped him ascend as Roman Emperor.
The tyrant Caligula was murdered less than four years into his reign. He was also locked in the underground of the destroyed altar by enraged and fearful mobs, forced to endure hunger and thirst for nearly a thousand years.
Throughout the long years, he survived by feeding on the blood of rats, the thirst inherited from the depths of his bloodline driving him mad.
Not enough, far from enough...
During the @2 period, a Levantine wizard found him, promising to help him escape, provided he killed Lemuel warriors on the battlefield.
What a great deal!
He completed the task with ease, but there was a small hiccup: in his excitement, he accidentally killed everyone on the battlefield and drank their blood — including the wizard and himself.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
@2 Years, the ancient city of Akka.
After a millennium of hunger and thirst, it finally erupted—killing, drinking blood, killing, drinking blood. In the end, all that remained before him was an endless desert and countless dried corpses. Even vultures feared to approach him.
Kneeling in the yellow sand, he laughed heartily: Exhilarating! Truly @1 exhilarating!
Now, he himself would bring this massacre to a perfect end.
The warrior bit his wrist, drained his own blood, and collapsed into the crimson pool.
